title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Spiral Review
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a revision-query and change-discovery workbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.
Interpret two-dot, three-dot, negative revisions, symmetric difference, and command-specific diff behavior.
Use pathspec magic, exclusions, attributes, top-level anchoring, and separator placement without confusing paths with revisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Revision Names evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Revision Names assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Revision Names has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Revision Names precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Feature tip F has 5 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: HEAD~2 and HEAD^2 always select the same commit.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Change Review Ranges: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Feature tip F has 2 commits after merge base M while main has two. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose a review range that matches branch intent, merge base, stacked work, and force-push risk.
